issues_classified.description: H4 causal conclusion 'dictionary completeness — not absorption level — drives patching fidelity' remains in Abstract, Section 5.3, Section 6.3, and Conclusion for 13+ iterations. The experiment compares full SAE (100%) vs 10% subsets — tests reconstruction capacity, NOT absorption's causal role. Both absorption subsets yield 0.0. The paper correctly labels H4 as 'uninformative' but the causal conclusion contradicts this label. No conclusion about absorption level predicting circuit importance can be drawn when both subsets yield 0.0.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove all variants of the causal conclusion from Abstract, Section 5.3, Section 6.3, and Conclusion. Replace with: 'H4 is inconclusive: both absorption subsets yield 0.0 faithfulness, preventing any conclusion about whether absorption level predicts circuit-level causal importance. Full SAE patching (0.289) outperforms subset patching (0.000), but this tests dictionary completeness (reconstruction capacity), not absorption quality. The correctly designed experiment (comparing full SAE representations at layers with different absorption profiles) was not conducted. H4 is inconclusive, not falsified.' Estimated effort: 30 minutes writing edit.

issues_classified.description: 8 perfect-score latents (Af=1.0) each fire on exactly 100 tokens — exactly matching the number of sequences in the pilot corpus (100 sequences). h5_pilot_output.log confirms this pattern at ALL dictionary sizes (2K, 8K, 24K). The regularity is too systematic to be coincidence. Paper treats as 'open question' in Section 6.5 but the regularity is dispositive of a positional artifact. If confirmed as positional artifacts, the Af>0.5 threshold interpretation at high absorption levels is invalidated. CPU analysis only, ~2 hours, zero resource barrier.
issues_classified.suggestion: Investigate immediately: (1) Compute token position consistency across sequences for each perfect-score latent — if all fire at the same position X across all 100 sequences, this confirms positional artifact; (2) Check whether encoder outputs exceed threshold only at that consistent position; (3) If confirmed as artifacts, exclude from primary analysis, document in Appendix with quantified impact on absorption statistics. CPU analysis only, ~2 hours. Do not leave as 'open question' — the regularity is dispositive.

issues_classified.description: H2 (token frequency correlation) was never tested for 13+ iterations. Layer 4 has 49.3% absorption (~12,000 latents) — 260x more data than layer 8 (46 latents). H3 data already collected at layer 4 — no new experiments needed, only CPU analysis (~2 hours). The decision to skip H2 at layer 4 was never documented with an explicit justification.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either: (a) Run H2 at layer 4 (49.3% absorption, ~12,000 latents provides ample statistical power for Spearman correlation) using existing H3 data — CPU analysis only, ~2 hours, no new experiments needed; OR (b) formally retire H2 with explicit justification: 'H2 was not tested at layer 8 due to insufficient absorption variance (46/24,576 latents). Layer 4 (49.3% absorption, ~12,000 latents) was identified as the appropriate alternative but the experiment was not conducted. H2 is formally retired: insufficient statistical power even at the highest-absorption layer tested.'

issues_classified.description: Section 3.1 partial reconstruction formula omits b_dec (decoder bias), contradicting notation.md which includes it. While the paper claims b_dec cancels in the RVE ratio, this cancellation is not proven in the text. Reviewers will flag this inconsistency. Pilot review gate failed to catch this before supervisor review. Estimated fix: 30 minutes.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add b_dec to the formula in Section 3.1 to match notation.md, OR add an explicit algebraic derivation showing that b_dec cancels in the Var(x_t - x_t_partial) / Var(x_t) ratio. The current presentation leaves the cancellation as an unproven assertion.

issues_classified.description: Section 5.2 calls layer 8 'the sparsest layer' but layer 8 has L0=71.9 (the highest in the dataset), meaning it has the MOST non-zero activations per token (LEAST sparse). Higher L0 = more non-zero = less sparse. This mischaracterization appears in multiple sections and contradicts Table 2 data.
issues_classified.suggestion: Change 'the sparsest layer' to 'the layer with the highest L0 (least sparse representation)' or 'the densest layer' consistently throughout the paper. Layer 8 has the highest L0=71.9, meaning it is the least sparse (most dense) layer in the dataset. Estimated fix: 30 minutes.

issues_classified.description: Section 5.5 contains inappropriate editorializing: 'the decision to not run H2 was not data-driven. A principled decision framework requires either running H2 at layer 4... or formally retiring H2 with explicit justification.' This reads as peer review criticism of the authors' own prior decisions rather than scientific reporting. Pilot review gate failed to catch this.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove the editorializing. Restructure as: 'H2 was not tested due to insufficient absorption variance at layer 8 (46 of 24,576 latents with $A_f > 0.5$). However, layer 4 has 49.3% absorption (~12,000 latents) — 260x more data than layer 8. A redesigned H2 experiment should use layer 4 as the primary test layer, where absorption variance is sufficient for correlation analysis.' Frame the limitation directly without characterizing the prior decision as wrong.

issues_classified.description: H1 hypothesis verdict is internally inconsistent. The hypothesis predicts '>20% of latents at layers 4-10' have Af > 0.5. At layer 8: 0.19% (falsified). At layer 4: 49.3% (CONFIRMS the hypothesis). Table 1 labels H1 as 'Falsified' which is only true for layer 8; layer 4 is NOT falsified. The abstract states 'falsifying the hypothesis at that layer' which is accurate for layer 8, but the paper does not adequately address why layer 4's 49.3% does not contradict the falsification claim.
issues_classified.suggestion: Restate H1 verdict as layer-dependent in Abstract, Section 5.1, and Table 1: 'H1 is falsified at layer 8 (0.19%, below 10% threshold) but confirmed at layer 4 (49.3%, exceeding >20% threshold). Results show strong layer-dependence requiring explanation. The hypothesis cannot be cleanly labeled as falsified when one layer confirms and another contradicts.' Use 'confirmed at layer 4' not 'not falsified at layer 4'.

issues_classified.description: Only pilot experiments (n=100) reported, not full-scale (n=1,024) as stated in methodology. The 10x scale gap is material for rare phenomenon characterization. No commitment to either pilot scope or full-scale replication has been made.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either: (a) Run full-scale experiments (n=1,024) with pre-committed analysis plan; OR (b) Explicitly scope the paper as a pilot study in Abstract and Discussion with commitment to full-scale replication before publication. This decision must be made before next supervisor review.

issues_classified.description: No seed ablation for any experiment. All experiments use seed=42 only. The dramatic layer discrepancy (layer 4 at 49.3% vs layer 8 at 0.19%) is too large to trust without seed confirmation. If layer-dependence is a seed artifact, the primary finding is invalid.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add seed ablation (seeds 42, 43, 44) for key findings: H1 layer comparison (layer 4 vs layer 8) and H3 inverted-U pattern. Report variance across seeds. Estimated time: ~4 hours CPU. If not feasible before next milestone, explicitly acknowledge as limitation in the paper.

issues_classified.description: Sections 5.6 and 5.7 are redundant: Table 1 in Section 5.6 already summarizes all 5 hypotheses with falsification status. Section 5.7 then repeats the layer-by-layer breakdown in Table 2. This adds length without new information.
issues_classified.suggestion: Integrate Table 2 into Section 5.6 as a second table, or move Section 5.7 to the Appendix and keep only the summary table in Section 5.6.

issues_classified.description: Figure 2 description mismatch: Text says 'Figure 2 shows the detailed absorption score distribution for layer 4' but Figure 2 shows the inverted-U pattern across all 6 layers. The layer-4 histogram is Figure 4.
issues_classified.suggestion: Fix Section 5.2 figure reference: change 'Figure 2' to 'Figure 4' for layer-4 histogram description. Alternatively, update text to describe what Figure 2 actually shows (inverted-U across all layers).

issues_classified.description: Abstract line 7: 'only 0.19% do' does not specify the layer. A reader could incorrectly interpret this as a model-wide figure rather than a layer-specific result.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add 'at layer 8' after 'only 0.19%' in the abstract to clarify this is a layer-specific result.

issues_classified.description: W_enc, b_enc, and lambda appear in paper body without definitions. Glossary defines them but paper text does not reference the definitions. A reader unfamiliar with the notation table would not know what these symbols represent.
issues_classified.suggestion: Define W_enc and b_enc explicitly in Section 2.1 when first describing SAE architecture. Define lambda in Section 3 when first using it in the absorption score formula.

issues_classified.description: Section 6.4 states 'approximately 79% of latents have Af <= 0.5' at layer 8. From Table 2: 20.9% have Af > 0.5 and 76.8% have Af = 0.0, accounting for 97.7%. The 79% figure (100 - 20.9) conflates 'not clearly absorbed' with 'completely independent' — these are distinct categories.
issues_classified.suggestion: Clarify decomposition: 'At layer 8, 20.9% of latents show Af > 0.5 (absorbed), 76.8% show Af = 0.0 (completely independent), and 2.3% show 0 < Af <= 0.5 (partial absorption). The two clean categories account for 97.7% of latents.'

Random dictionary validation correctly distinguishes real SAE from random controls (0.00% at all dictionary sizes)
Inverted-U finding (absorption peaks at layer 4 at 49.3%) is genuine and worth publishing as a negative result
Section 3 (The Absorption Score) is the paper's strongest technical writing
All pilot experiments (H1-H5) completed successfully with structured outputs
Validated absorption metric (Af) with random dictionary control is sound and reproducible
Writing quality improved to 8/10 — execution is working at the writing level
All 5 figures exist in writing/figures/ — do NOT regenerate
H1 messaging improved: layer 8 (0.19%) and layer 4 (49.3%) both reported correctly
H4 correctly labeled as uninformative: Paper explicitly acknowledges correct experiment never conducted

No escalation mechanism when same issues persist across 13+ iterations
